conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: We need a vector database for our semantic search. Let's use Pinecone. It's fully managed and fast.

conversation.speaker: assistant
conversation.text: Pinecone is the leading option. I'll set up the indexes.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Pinecone is expensive. Let's use Weaviate. It's open-source and can be self-hosted.

conversation.speaker: assistant
conversation.text: Weaviate has GraphQL support. I'll deploy it on our Kubernetes cluster.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Actually, we need to do hybrid search. Qdrant supports dense and sparse vectors. Let's use Qdrant.

conversation.speaker: assistant
conversation.text: Qdrant is written in Rust. It's very performant. I'll set up the collection.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: We want to use the same database as our main application. Let's use PostgreSQL with pgvector. It's simple and integrated.

conversation.speaker: assistant
conversation.text: pgvector is easy to use. I'll add the extension and create the tables.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Actually, our search queries are very complex. We need dedicated indexing. Let's use Qdrant. It has the best filtering capabilities.

query: What vector database is the team using for semantic search?
